rationale: The citing paper centrally models governance practices such as monitoring and tests effects via perceived selling-firm opportunism, which closely aligns with the cited paper’s core constructs (monitoring forms, opportunism, and formal/informal governance interactions). The citing note explicitly says it extends prior dyadic trust/governance work into a multiparty platform context, making this 2007 interfirm monitoring study a plausible theory/mechanism foundation rather than an incidental mention.
